en.extract:
The plumbeous hawk (Cryptoleucopteryx plumbea) is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae belonging to the monotypic genus Cryptoleucopteryx. It ranges 33 to 37 cm in length and 71 to 79 cm in wingspan.


Systematics
The plumbeous hawk was described by English naturalist Osbert Salvin in 1872 under the protonym Leucopternis plumbea. As the genus Leucopternis is masculine in gender, the scientific name was revised to Leucopternis plumbeus to meet the International Code of Zoological Nomenclature.


Distribution
The plumbeous hawk is found in Colombia, Ecuador, Panama, and northwestern Peru. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It can also be found on silvopastures. The population is threatened by habitat loss resulting from agriculture, logging, and mining.


References


External links

 Data related to Plumbeous hawk at Wikispecies

The '''plumbeous hawk'''<!-- , '''Buse Plombée''', '''Busardo Plomizo''', or '''Gavilán Plomizo'''--> ('''''Cryptoleucopteryx plumbea''''') is a species of [[bird of prey]] in the family [[Accipitridae]] belonging to the [[Monotypic taxon|monotypic]] genus '''''Cryptoleucopteryx'''''. It ranges 33 to 37 cm in length and 71 to 79 cm in wingspan.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ferguson-Lees |first=James |title=Raptors of the World |date=2005 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing Plc |others=David A. Christie |isbn=978-0-7136-6957-2 |series=Helm Field Guides |location=London}}</ref>

== Systematics ==
The plumbeous hawk was described by English naturalist [[Osbert Salvin]] in 1872 under the [[protonym]] ''Leucopternis plumbea''.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Salvin |first=Osbert |date=1872 |title=XXIX.—Further Revision of the Genus Leucopternis, with a Description of a new Species |url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1474-919X.1872.tb08406.x |journal=Ibis |language=en |volume=14 |issue=3 |pages=239–243 |doi=10.1111/j.1474-919X.1872.tb08406.x |issn=0019-1019 |via=Biodiversity Heritage Library}}</ref> As the genus ''[[Leucopternis]]'' is masculine in gender, the scientific name was revised to ''Leucopternis plumbeus'' to meet the [[International Code of Zoological Nomenclature#Gender agreement|International Code of Zoological Nomenclature]].<ref>{{Cite journal |last=David |first=Normand |last2=Gosselin |first2=Michel |date=2002 |title=The Grammatical Gender of Avian Genera |url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/part/151171 |journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists’ Club |volume=122 |pages=257–282 |via=Biodiversity Heritage Library}}</ref>

==Distribution==
The plumbeous hawk is found in [[Colombia]], [[Ecuador]], [[Panama]], and northwestern [[Peru]]. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It can also be found on [[silvopasture]]s.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Myster |first=Randall W. |date=2015-04-23 |title=Seed predation, pathogens and germination in primary vs. secondary cloud forest at Maquipucuna Reserve, Ecuador |journal=Journal of Tropical Ecology |volume=31 |issue=4 |pages=375–378 |doi=10.1017/s0266467415000127 |issn=0266-4674}}</ref> The population is threatened by [[habitat loss]] resulting from agriculture, logging, and mining.<ref name="iucn status 30 April 2026" />
